pages.title: Welcome
pages.body:
# Welcome to Docs

This is a **rich-text** notebook. Every page in the tree on the left is a full document — type below, use the toolbar for **bold**, *italic*, headings, lists and links, then tap the eye to preview.

Pages nest as deep as you like: a page can hold child pages (tabs → sub-tabs → sub-sub-tabs), so you can organise anything from a quick note to a whole handbook.

## Try it

- Tap **New page** to add a top-level page
- Open a page and tap the **+** to give it a child page
- Drag-hold a page to re-nest it under another

pages.title: Getting started
pages.body:
## Getting started

1. Pages live in a **tree** — the page you are reading is a child of *Welcome*.
2. Each page stores its own rich-text body, independent of its parent.
3. Attach files or images to any page from the editor's **Attachments** field.

pages.title: Formatting
pages.body:
## Formatting

The editor speaks Markdown:

- **bold** with `**double asterisks**`
- *italic* with `*single asterisks*`
- `## Heading` for a section heading
- `- item` for a bullet list
- `[label](https://example.com)` for a link

This page itself has a child — open **Checklists** below it to see sub-sub-tab nesting.

pages.title: Checklists
pages.body:
### Checklists

You can keep checklists as a Markdown list today:

- [ ] Draft the outline
- [ ] Write the first section
- [x] Set up the page tree

> Tickable in-doc checkboxes are a planned engine upgrade; for now the boxes render as text you edit by hand.

pages.title: My Project
pages.body:
# My Project

A second top-level page, to show that the tree holds several independent documents side by side. Add your own children under here.

pages.title: Notes
pages.body:
## Notes

Free-form notes for the project.
